title: Getting started with biooracler
text: Bio-Oracle provides marine environmental layers (temperature, salinity, nutrients, ice cover, ) present-day conditions future climate-change projections. data served ERDDAP server hosted Flanders Marine Institute (VLIZ). biooracler thin wrapper around rerddap makes convenient search, inspect, download layers R.
code: library(biooracler)

title: Getting started with biooracler
text: Every dataset server unique dataset_id. Use list_layers() see available, pass free-text query narrow search. naming dataset_id encodes variable, time period, depth, example thetao_ssp585_2020_2100_depthsurf (ocean temperature, SSP5-8.5 scenario, 2020-2100, surface). See ?dataset_id full convention.
code: # All layers list_layers()  # Free-text search list_layers("Ocean Temperature")

title: Getting started with biooracler
text: info_layer() returns metadata one dataset, including variables ranges dimensions (time, latitude, longitude). Use find valid constraint values downloading.
code: info_layer("thetao_baseline_2000_2019_depthsurf")

title: Getting started with biooracler
text: download_layers() main function. give dataset_id, variables want, list constraints time, latitude, longitude dimensions. default result terra::SpatRaster, ready mapping analysis:
code: dataset_id <- "thetao_baseline_2000_2019_depthsurf"  constraints <- list(   time      = c("2000-01-01T00:00:00Z", "2010-01-01T00:00:00Z"),   latitude  = c(40, 50),   longitude = c(-10, 0) )  layers <- download_layers(   dataset_id,   variables   = c("thetao_mean"),   constraints = constraints )  layers terra::plot(layers)

title: Getting started with biooracler
text: fmt argument controls return type: "raster" (default): terra::SpatRaster. "nc": parsed NetCDF object rerddap. "csv": data.frame values.
code: df <- download_layers(dataset_id, "thetao_mean", constraints, fmt = "csv") head(df)

title: Getting started with biooracler
text: Downloads cached disk. Pass directory choose , filename give file human-readable name instead default cache hash.
code: download_layers(   dataset_id, "thetao_mean", constraints,   directory = tempdir(),   filename  = "thetao_baseline_NE_atlantic" )

what: A note on the time constraint
title: Getting started with biooracler
text: time constraint range: every time step falls within returned separate layer. Decadal baseline datasets store snapshots decade boundaries, range spans two snapshots returns two layers. get single time step, set start end time value.

title: Getting started with biooracler
text: ?download_layers, ?list_layers, ?info_layer function reference. ?dataset_id dataset naming convention. Bio-Oracle website science behind data.

title: Import Environmental Ocean Data from Bio-Oracle via ERDDAP
text: First need define dataset download together time, latitude longitude constrains. constains must provided named list containing least one : time, latitude longitude. Define also variables download: Perform download can modify download path output type arguments directory fmt respectively.
code: dataset_id <- "tas_baseline_2000_2020_depthsurf"  time = c('2001-01-01T00:00:00Z', '2010-01-01T00:00:00Z') latitude = c(10, 20) longitude = c(120, 130)  constraints = list(time, latitude, longitude) names(constraints) = c("time", "latitude", "longitude") variables = c("tas_max", "tas_min") layers <- download_layers(dataset_id, variables, constraints) #> Selected dataset tas_baseline_2000_2020_depthsurf. #> Dataset info available at: http://erddap.bio-oracle.org/erddap/griddap/tas_baseline_2000_2020_depthsurf.html #> Selected 2 variables: tas_max, tas_min dir <- tempdir() download_layers(dataset_id, variables, constraints, fmt = "csv", directory = dir) #> Selected dataset tas_baseline_2000_2020_depthsurf. #> Dataset info available at: http://erddap.bio-oracle.org/erddap/griddap/tas_baseline_2000_2020_depthsurf.html #> Selected 2 variables: tas_max, tas_min #> <ERDDAP griddap> tas_baseline_2000_2020_depthsurf #>    Path: [/tmp/RtmpXUWMRn/8774b92c003f15b9f929254b0341496f.csv] #>    Last updated: [2024-03-04 10:49:55.735117] #>    File size:    [5.59 mb] #>    Dimensions:   [80802 X 5] #>  #> # A tibble: 80,802 × 5 #>    time                 latitude longitude tas_max tas_min #>    <chr>                   <dbl>     <dbl>   <dbl>   <dbl> #>  1 2000-01-01T00:00:00Z     10.0      120.    29.5    27.1 #>  2 2000-01-01T00:00:00Z     10.0      120.    29.5    27.1 #>  3 2000-01-01T00:00:00Z     10.0      120.    29.5    27.1 #>  4 2000-01-01T00:00:00Z     10.0      120.    29.5    27.1 #>  5 2000-01-01T00:00:00Z     10.0      120.    29.6    27.2 #>  6 2000-01-01T00:00:00Z     10.0      120.    29.6    27.2 #>  7 2000-01-01T00:00:00Z     10.0      120.    29.6    27.2 #>  8 2000-01-01T00:00:00Z     10.0      120.    29.6    27.2 #>  9 2000-01-01T00:00:00Z     10.0      120.    29.6    27.2 #> 10 2000-01-01T00:00:00Z     10.0      120.    29.6    27.2 #> # ℹ 80,792 more rows   download_layers(dataset_id, variables, constraints, fmt = "raster", directory = dir) #> Selected dataset tas_baseline_2000_2020_depthsurf. #> Dataset info available at: http://erddap.bio-oracle.org/erddap/griddap/tas_baseline_2000_2020_depthsurf.html #> Selected 2 variables: tas_max, tas_min #> class       : SpatRaster  #> dimensions  : 201, 201, 4  (nrow, ncol, nlyr) #> resolution  : 0.04999996, 0.05  (x, y) #> extent      : 120, 130.05, 10, 20.05  (xmin, xmax, ymin, ymax) #> coord. ref. : lon/lat WGS 84  #> sources     : f9db8428abb69301c6f80584b16feaf5.nc:tas_max  (2 layers)  #>               f9db8428abb69301c6f80584b16feaf5.nc:tas_min  (2 layers)  #> varnames    : tas_max (Maximum AirTemperature)  #>               tas_min (Minimum AirTemperature)  #> names       : tas_max_1, tas_max_2, tas_min_1, tas_min_2  #> unit        :         K,         K,         K,         K  #> time        : 2000-01-01 to 2010-01-01 UTC

title: Bio-Oracle ERDDAP Server — ERDDAP
text: Bio-Oracle dataset made available via ERDDAP server hosted Flanders Marine Institute (VLIZ). ERDDAP data server gives simple, consistent way download subsets scientific datasets common file formats make graphs maps. Bio-Oracle ERDDAP installation available online : https://erddap.bio-oracle.org/erddap/ ERDDAP offers REST API can consumed software instance, R package reads data ERDDAP named rerddap::rerddap. package backbone behind biooracler: fact, biooracler just wraps rerddap::rerddap convenient way read data Bio-Oracle ERDDAP server. Bio-Oracle ERDDAP server independent biooracler package. welcome access Bio-Oracle ERDDAP server preferred way, including via rerddap::rerddap R package. ease combination Bio-Oracle dataset several ERDDAP servers. addition, Python extension mimics methods biooracler. can access : https://github.com/bio-oracle/pyo_oracle/ dataset Bio-Oracle ERDDAP server identified dataset_id.

what: Unique Identifier of a Bio-Oracle dataset in the ERDDAP Server — dataset_id
title: Unique Identifier of a Bio-Oracle dataset in the ERDDAP Server — dataset_id
text: Bio-Oracle datasets unique identifier ERDDAP server hosted. Despite title summary dataset (See list_layers()) already describe data included dataset, dataset_id follow certain naming indicate information included inside. instance: phyc_baseline_2000_2020_depthsurf thetao_ssp119_2020_2100_depthmean thetao_baseline_2000_2019_depthsurf

title: Unique Identifier of a Bio-Oracle dataset in the ERDDAP Server — dataset_id
text: Indicated first part naming (e.g. phyc, thetao). equivalences : chl = Chlorophyll clt = Total Cloud Fraction dfe = Dissolved Iron mlotst = Mixed Layer Depth no3 = Nitrate o2 = Dissolved Molecular Oxygen ph = pH phyc = Total Phytoplankton po4 = Phosphate si = Silicate siconc = Sea Ice Cover sithick = Sea Ice Thickness = Salinity sws = Sea Water Speed tas = Air Temperature terrain = Terrain thetao = Ocean Temperature

what: Time: Present/Past baseline or Future CMIIP6 Climate Change Projection projection
title: Unique Identifier of a Bio-Oracle dataset in the ERDDAP Server — dataset_id
text: part baseline ssp119 indicates layer contains present past decades, future climate change projections provisions Intergovernmental Panel Climate Change (IPCC) 6th report. report features 6th generation Coupled Model Intercomparison Projects (CMIP6) describes Shared Socioeconomic Pathways (SSPs), different climate change scenarios. Bio-Oracle dataset predicts environmental variables 8 future decades (2020-2100) 6 SSPs scenarios: ssp119, ssp126, ssp245, ssp370, ssp460 ssp585. hand, baseline refers conditions environmental variable decades 2000 2010 2010 2020, interpolated Copernicus Marine Service data. named baseline interpolated layers baseline model future predictions.

title: Unique Identifier of a Bio-Oracle dataset in the ERDDAP Server — dataset_id
text: Bio-Oracle datasets includes modelled environmental data surface sea water layer (depthsurf), also bottom ocean. Note datasets subfixes depthmin, depthmean depthmax refer benthic layer never refer different depths ocean. words: Bio-Oracle offers data ocean surface bottom, never different levels depth. subfixes min, mean max refer depth value used modelling: Bio-Oracle uses bathymetry data GEBCO, higher resolution resolution Bio-Oracle. Hence, grid cell Bio-Oracle dataset one value bathymetry Three different bathymetry values selected fit models: either minimal value (depthmin), average bathymetry values (depthmean) maximum value (depthmax).

title: Unique Identifier of a Bio-Oracle dataset in the ERDDAP Server — dataset_id
text: Returns help page

title: Unique Identifier of a Bio-Oracle dataset in the ERDDAP Server — dataset_id
code: if (FALSE) ?dataset_id # \dontrun{}

what: Downloads Bio-Oracle layers from the Bio-Oracle ERDDAP server — download_layers
title: Downloads Bio-Oracle layers from the Bio-Oracle ERDDAP server — download_layers
text: Downloads Bio-Oracle layers Bio-Oracle ERDDAP server

title: Downloads Bio-Oracle layers from the Bio-Oracle ERDDAP server — download_layers
code: download_layers(   dataset_id,   variables = NULL,   constraints = list(),   fmt = "raster",   directory = NULL,   filename = NULL,   verbose = TRUE,   debug = FALSE )

title: Downloads Bio-Oracle layers from the Bio-Oracle ERDDAP server — download_layers
text: dataset_id unique identifier dataset ERDDAP. See dataset_id. variables Variable names retrieve dataset. NULL (default), variables retrieved. constraints List query dimensions time, latitude longitude fmt Output format. One 'csv', 'nc' 'raster' (default). See details. directory Local directory files cached. NULL (default), rerddap::griddap filename Optional name (without extension) downloaded file. NULL (default), cryptic cache name rerddap::griddap kept. See details. verbose Logical: Show info messages? Default TRUE debug Logical: Print extra information helps debugging? Default FALSE

title: Downloads Bio-Oracle layers from the Bio-Oracle ERDDAP server — download_layers
text: downloaded csv nc file object class griddap_csv, griddap_nc SpatRast

title: Downloads Bio-Oracle layers from the Bio-Oracle ERDDAP server — download_layers
text: Bio-Oracle layers retrieved downloaded cache directory later read memory R. Depending output format selected fmt argument obtain: csv: object class griddap_csv. essence, data.frame extra info. csv file downloaded cache directory. See rerddap::griddap(). nc: object class griddap_nc. unclassed output ncdf4::nc_open(). NetCDF file downloaded cache directory. See rerddap::griddap(). raster: object class SpatRast, obtained reading NetCDF file terra::rast. default, downloaded files named cryptic hash (rerddap::griddap()). Pass filename save human-readable copy, e.g. filename = "chl_2040_ssp585". copy written directory (cache directory directory NULL) extension matching fmt. original cached file preserved caching still works. time constraint range: every time step falls within [start, end] interval returned separate layer. Decadal baseline datasets store snapshots decade boundaries, range like c('2000-01-01T00:00:00Z', '2010-01-01T00:00:00Z') spans two snapshots (two layers), c('2005-01-01T00:00:00Z', '2010-01-01T00:00:00Z') spans one (single layer). get single time step, set start end time value. ERDDAP reports coordinates cell centers. result, extent returned raster reaches half cell-width beyond nominal bounds (e.g. slightly past +/-180 longitude). expected: data covers full grid shifted.

title: Downloads Bio-Oracle layers from the Bio-Oracle ERDDAP server — download_layers
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Test variables dataset_id = "tas_baseline_2000_2020_depthsurf" time = c('2001-01-01T00:00:00Z', '2010-01-01T00:00:00Z') latitude = c(10, 20) longitude = c(120, 130) variables = c("tas_max", "tas_min") constraints = list(time, latitude, longitude) names(constraints) = c("time", "latitude", "longitude")  # Test call download_layers(dataset_id, variables, constraints) } # }

title: biooracler 1.0.0
text: First stable release.

title: biooracler 1.0.0
text: download_layers() gains filename argument save downloaded files human-readable name instead cryptic cache hash (#13).

title: biooracler 1.0.0
text: download_layers() now reports clearer error messages Bio-Oracle ERDDAP server unreachable constraints fall outside dataset range, warns query returns data (#3). Bio-Oracle ERDDAP server now contacted HTTPS.

title: biooracler 1.0.0
text: Clarified time constraint range (one layer per time step within , #18) raster extent reaches half cell beyond nominal bounds ERDDAP reports cell-center coordinates (#20). Fixed dataset_id reference (duplicate phyc entry transposed year example).

title: biooracler 1.0.0
text: Added GitHub Actions R-CMD-check workflow across Linux, macOS Windows. Added BugReports, tidied DESCRIPTION metadata, removed unused dependencies dead code.
